div.head: The Acting Representative to the Provisional Government of the French Republic at Algiers (Lawton) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
2307. From Murphy. The CinCCommander in Chief, Gen. Dwight D. Eisenhower. has requested advice at the instance of General EakerLt. Gen. Ira C. Baker, U.S. Army, Air Commander in Chief of Mediterranean Allied Air Force. regarding the status of the Republics of San Marino and Andorra and the Principalities of Monaco and Liechtenstein. General Eaker is particularly desirous of knowing
whether German troops and matériel passing through those states could be attacked from the air. Admiral CunninghamAdm. John Henry Dacres Cunningham, British Allied Naval Commander, Mediterranean Theater. also points out that Monaco is of capital importance because in addition to the port it is on the main road and railroad from Italy to France. Department’s guidance is requested. [Murphy.]
